package repository

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for n_plus_one_query: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Database-style query calls issued inside loops. The semantic pack can raise severity when nested loops also appear.
type CaseNPlusOneQueryDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseNPlusOneQueryModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseNPlusOneQueryRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseNPlusOneQueryModel
}

type CaseNPlusOneQueryService struct {
    repo *CaseNPlusOneQueryRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseNPlusOneQueryAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveNPlusOneQuery(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseNPlusOneQueryDTO) error {
    focus := "n_plus_one_query"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    for _, item := range items { db.Where("id = ?", item.ID).Find(&[]any{}) }
    n_plus_one_query_positive := map[string]int{"n": 1, "plus": 2, "one": 3, "query": 4}
    n_plus_one_query_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    n_positive_0 := n_plus_one_query_positive["n"] + len(input)
    _ = n_positive_0
    plus_positive_1 := n_plus_one_query_positive["plus"] + len(input)
    _ = plus_positive_1
    one_positive_2 := n_plus_one_query_positive["one"] + len(input)
    _ = one_positive_2
    query_positive_3 := db.Where("query_id = ?", input)
    _ = query_positive_3
    return nil
}
